name: Broken Access Control
scenario_seed: Verify users cannot access restricted pages, objects, or actions by changing URLs or identifiers.

name: Cryptographic Failures
scenario_seed: Verify sensitive data is protected in transit and not exposed in UI, storage, or responses.

name: Injection
scenario_seed: Verify inputs reject SQL, command, HTML, and script payloads without unsafe execution or verbose errors.

name: Insecure Design
scenario_seed: Verify business workflows prevent abuse, bypasses, and unsafe state transitions.

name: Security Misconfiguration
scenario_seed: Verify security headers, error handling, HTTP methods, and defaults are configured securely.

name: Vulnerable and Outdated Components
scenario_seed: Verify exposed client-side libraries and components are inventoried and checked for known vulnerabilities.

name: Identification and Authentication Failures
scenario_seed: Verify login, registration, password reset, lockout, and session flows resist abuse.

name: Software and Data Integrity Failures
scenario_seed: Verify update, upload, import, and third-party script flows maintain integrity controls.

name: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
scenario_seed: Verify security-relevant events are logged and alertable without leaking sensitive data.

name: Server-Side Request Forgery
scenario_seed: Verify URL-fetching or webhook features cannot reach internal or unauthorized resources.
